entry.title: Critical defect in Java security engine poses serious downstream security risks
entry.content:
Attackers can exploit the defect in the widely deployed pac4j with relative ease, but researchers haven’t observed active exploitation in the wild. The post Critical defect in Java security engine poses serious downstream security risks appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: FBI says even in an AI-powered world, security basics still matter
entry.content:
AI is speeding up attacks but otherwise not changing their fundamental nature, said FBI official Jason Bilnoski. The post FBI says even in an AI-powered world, security basics still matter appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Microsoft’s monthly Patch Tuesday is first in 6 months with no actively exploited zero-days
entry.content:
The vendor said six of the 83 vulnerabilities it addressed this month are more likely to be exploited. The post Microsoft’s monthly Patch Tuesday is first in 6 months with no actively exploited zero-days appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: LatAm Now Faces 2x More Cyberattacks Than US
entry.content:
Much of Central and South America struggles with cybersecurity maturity, and hackers are taking advantage.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Fig Security Emerges From Stealth to Fix Broken Security Operations
entry.content:
Fig Security's platform traces security data flows end-to-end across SIEMs, pipelines, and response systems to alert teams before infrastructure changes break critical defenses.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Tycoon 2FA Goes Boom as Europol, Vendors Bust Phishing Platform
entry.content:
The phishing-as-a-service platform was popular among cyber threat actors because of its ability to bypass multifactor authentication defenses.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Nation-State Actor Embraces AI Malware Assembly Line
entry.content:
Pakistan's APT36 threat group has begun using vibe-coding to churn out mediocre malware, but at a scale that could overwhelm defenses.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Cyberattack on Mexico's Gov't Agencies Highlight AI Threat
entry.content:
Using Anthropic's Claude, OpenAI's ChatGPT, and a detailed playbook prompt, a handful of cyberattackers reportedly gained access to government agencies and its citizens' data.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Iran's Cyber-Kinetic War Doctrine Takes Shape
entry.content:
Iran has been hacking IP cameras to plan missile strikes against its enemies, and mounting other attacks on physical assets, showing how cyber and kinetic warfare are fast becoming one and the same.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: EU Auto Rules Shift Gears on Cybersecurity Standards
entry.content:
The European Union is taking new precautions as climate change and cybersecurity threats rise across the automotive industry.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Cylake Offers AI-Native Security Without Relying on Cloud Services
entry.content:
Cylake's platform will analyze security data locally and identify potential attacks for organizations concerned about data sovereignty.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Chinese Cyber Threat Lurks In Critical Asian Sectors for Years
entry.content:
An undefined Chinese-speaking actor wields a combo of custom malware, open source tools, and LOTL binaries against Windows and Linux, likely for spying.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: 'InstallFix' Attacks Spread Fake Claude Code Sites
entry.content:
A fresh cyberattack campaign blends malvertising with a ClickFix-style technique that highlights risky behavior with AI coding assistants and command-line interfaces.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: White House Cyber Strategy Prioritizes Offense
entry.content:
In a seven-page strategy document, the Trump administration signaled a shift to preemption and deterrence to handling cyber threats.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Russian Threat Actor Sednit Resurfaces With Sophisticated Toolkit
entry.content:
After several years of using simple implants, the Russia-affiliated actor is back with two new sophisticated malware tools.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Hundreds of Salesforce Customers Allegedly Targeted in New Data Theft Campaign
entry.content:
Salesforce has confirmed that customers are being targeted via poorly secured instances. The post Hundreds of Salesforce Customers Allegedly Targeted in New Data Theft Campaign appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: OpenAI Rolls Out Codex Security Vulnerability Scanner
entry.content:
Codex Security, formerly Aardvark⁠, has found hundreds of critical vulnerabilities in tested software in the past month. The post OpenAI Rolls Out Codex Security Vulnerability Scanner appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Thousands Affected by Ericsson Data Breach
entry.content:
The telecommunications equipment and services giant has blamed the incident on a third-party vendor. The post Thousands Affected by Ericsson Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Webinar Today: Securing Fragile OT in an Exposed World
entry.content:
Join the webinar as we examine the current OT threat landscape and move past the "doom and gloom" to discuss the mechanics of modern OT exposure. The post Webinar Today: Securing Fragile OT in an Exposed World appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Kai Emerges From Stealth With $125M in Funding for AI Platform Bridging IT and OT Security
entry.content:
The company was created by a Claroty founder and is backed by Evolution Equity Partners, N47, and other investors. The post Kai Emerges From Stealth With $125M in Funding for AI Platform Bridging IT and OT Security appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Cybercriminals impersonating city officials to steal permit payments, FBI says
entry.content:
In a notice on Monday, the agency said people and businesses with active applications for the permits are being targeted with phishing emails that often include detailed, accurate information “including property addresses, case numbers, and the true names of city and county officials.”
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Finnish intelligence warns of persistent cyber espionage from Russia, China
entry.content:
Cyberespionage remains the country’s most significant digital threat, with attackers targeting government systems, research institutions and companies developing advanced technologies, according to a new intel report.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Rudd confirmed to head NSA, Cyber Command after near year-long vacancy
entry.content:
Rudd, who was confirmed 71-29 to serve as the “dual-hat” leader of the organizations, takes the reins as the U.S. faces mounting aggression in cyberspace from foreign adversaries at the same time the Trump administration has sought to shrink the size of the federal government.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
entry.content:
Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
entry.content:
AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and n
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Google: Cloud attacks exploit flaws more than weak credentials
entry.content:
Hackers are increasingly exploiting newly disclosed vulnerabilities in third-party software to gain initial access to cloud environments, with the window for attacks shrinking from weeks to just days. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: APT28 hackers deploy customized variant of Covenant open-source tool
entry.content:
The Russian state-sponsored APT28 threat group is using a custom variant of the open-source Covenant post-exploitation framework for long-term espionage operations. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: General Physician Pays $2.5 Million to Settle Data Breach Litigation
entry.content:
General Physician, P.C., a medical group serving patients in Western New York, has agreed to pay $2.5 million to settle […] The post General Physician Pays $2.5 Million to Settle Data Breach Litigation appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: PIH Health Notifies Patients About 2024 Hacking Incident
entry.content:
PIH Health, a healthcare provider serving patients in Orange County and the San Gabriel Valley in California, has started notifying […] The post PIH Health Notifies Patients About 2024 Hacking Incident appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: $2.35 Million Settlement Agreed to Resolve Cornerstone Specialty Hospitals Data Breach Lawsuit
entry.content:
Cornerstone Healthcare Group Management Services, doing business as Cornerstone Specialty Hospitals (Cornerstone), has agreed to settle class action litigation stemming […] The post $2.35 Million Settlement Agreed to Resolve Cornerstone Specialty Hospitals Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Business Associate Settles HIPAA Violations Related to Unreported Breach Affecting 15 Million Individuals
entry.content:
The Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) Office for Civil Rights (OCR) has announced its second enforcement action of […] The post Business Associate Settles HIPAA Violations Related to Unreported Breach Affecting 15 Million Individuals appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Alabama Hospital Recently Informed About 2024 Data Breach
entry.content:
Jackson Hospital and Clinic in Montgomery, Alabama, has notified 14,485 individuals about a July 2024 data breach at one of […] The post Alabama Hospital Recently Informed About 2024 Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: February 2025 Cyberattack Affected More Than 230K Bell Ambulance Patients
entry.content:
Bell Ambulance has confirmed that the protected health information of more than 230,000 patients was compromised in a February 2025 […] The post February 2025 Cyberattack Affected More Than 230K Bell Ambulance Patients appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.content:

📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach
